User can copy or bookmark the URL at this state; returning to it later will reload the same filters, sort, and page.

user_workflows.name: View details for a specific scholarship
steps: User identifies a scholarship of interest in the results list.
User clicks either the scholarship name or a dedicated "View details" action within that scholarship card.
The click handler reads the scholarship's id and constructs a navigation URL to scholarship_detail.html with a scholarshipId query parameter set to the selected id.
Browser navigates to scholarship_detail.html?scholarshipId=<id>, where the detail page is responsible for loading and displaying complete scholarship information and any saving functionality.

user_workflows.name: Navigate to support student scholarships (giving CTA)
steps: User sees a giving call-to-action related to supporting student scholarships, when the implementation chooses to render this component.
User clicks the giving CTA.

The click handler constructs a URL to give.html including a fund_id parameter referencing the student scholarships fund identifier provided by the implementation (e.g., through server-side rendering or a configuration value).
Browser navigates to give.html?fund_id=<fund_id>, where the giving page will use the fund_id to pre-select the appropriate fund.

interactions.description: Updates the eligibility_group state, writes eligibility_group to the URL query parameters, resets page to the first page, and triggers a new searchScholarships call with the updated filters.
Updates the level_of_study state, writes level_of_study to the URL query parameters, resets page to the first page, and triggers searchScholarships with the updated filters.

Updates the min_deadline state if the selected date falls within the deadline_range boundaries from getScholarshipFilterOptions; writes min_deadline (ISO date) to the URL query parameters, resets page, and triggers searchScholarships with the updated filters.
Updates the sort_by state, writes sort_by to the URL, resets page to the first page, and triggers searchScholarships with existing filters and the new sort_by value.
Updates the page state and page URL parameter while keeping the current filters and sort_by; triggers searchScholarships with the new page value.

interactions.element: Scholarship card name link or "View details" link
interactions.description: Reads the associated scholarship id and navigates to scholarship_detail.html?scholarshipId=<id>.
Navigates to give.html?fund_id=<configured_student_scholarships_fund_id> using a fund ID provided at render time by the implementation.

On page load, reads URL parameters (eligibility_group, level_of_study, min_deadline, sort_by, page), calls getScholarshipFilterOptions, validates and normalizes the parameters against available options, then calls searchScholarships with the resulting filters, sort_by, and page.

page_functionality.state_logic:
The Scholarships & Support page treats URL query parameters as the single source of truth for state. The URL may include eligibility_group, level_of_study, min_deadline, sort_by, and page. On initial load and on every state change, the page controller parses the URL, normalizes state, and then calls getScholarshipFilterOptions followed by searchScholarships.

If eligibility_group is present in the URL and matches one of the values returned in getScholarshipFilterOptions.eligibility_groups, it is used as the initial eligibility filter; otherwise, eligibility_group is omitted from the filters object passed to searchScholarships. The same pattern applies to level_of_study and getScholarshipFilterOptions.levels_of_study, and to sort_by and getScholarshipFilterOptions.sort_options.

The min_deadline URL parameter is expected to be an ISO date string. If provided and within the earliest and latest bounds returned in getScholarshipFilterOptions.deadline_range (or otherwise accepted by back-end validation), it is passed as filters.min_deadline to searchScholarships. If invalid, it is ignored or sanitized per implementation rules, and the URL can be updated to reflect the normalized state (e.g., removing the invalid parameter).

The page parameter is expected to be a positive integer. If missing or invalid, the implementation defaults to the first page. Whenever any filter or sort_by changes, the page parameter is reset to the first page to avoid empty-results scenarios due to pagination. Page changes always update the page parameter in the URL and trigger a new searchScholarships call with unchanged filters and sort_by.

The filters object passed to searchScholarships is constructed dynamically from the current state: it includes only the parameters that are present and valid (e.g., filters.eligibility_group, filters.level_of_study, filters.min_deadline). sort_by and page are passed as separate arguments, along with a page_size controlled by implementation configuration (not hard-coded in this specification). Any update to filters or sort_by results in updating the corresponding URL parameters and re-invoking searchScholarships, ensuring the URL always reflects the current view. This enables back/forward browser navigation and easy sharing or bookmarking of specific filter/sort/page combinations.

components.functionality: Coordinates initial data loading, URL parameter parsing, state normalization, and communication with getScholarshipFilterOptions and searchScholarships. Orchestrates data flow between filter controls, results list, and pagination.

Displays and manages scholarship filter controls for eligibility group, level of study, and minimum deadline, as well as the sort-by control. Reads available options from getScholarshipFilterOptions and synchronizes current selections with URL parameters. Notifies the page controller when the filters or sort option change.

Renders the list of scholarships returned by searchScholarships for the current page, showing key data such as scholarship name, eligibility group, level of study, award amount information, and deadline. Provides clickable elements (name or dedicated link) to navigate to scholarship_detail.html for each scholarship.

Displays pagination controls based on the total_results, page_size, and current page from searchScholarships. Allows users to move between result pages while keeping the current filters and sort. Updates the page URL parameter and triggers new searches via the page controller.

Optional component that renders a call-to-action link or button to support student scholarships. When activated, it navigates to give.html with a fund_id parameter corresponding to the student scholarships fund, using a fund identifier provided at render time (e.g., via server-side configuration).

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column desktop grid with a full-width header and navigation, and a centered content column spanning approximately 6–8 columns with large side gutters.
layout.layout_pattern: Below the existing global red utility bar and black main navigation (unchanged from the mockup), the Scholarships & Support page uses a single, centered content column that behaves like a formal editorial body. The flow is linear, top-to-bottom: first a concise page controller block (page title + high-level controls), followed by a compact filter bar, then a vertically stacked scholarship list. A single-line support CTA appears as a distinct, standalone line toward the lower third of the content column, and the pagination sits at the very bottom. There are no sidebars; all interactive elements stay within this central column to preserve the focused, letter-like reading experience.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Below the existing global red utility bar and black main navigation (unchanged from the mockup), the Scholarships & Support page uses a single, centered content column that behaves like a formal editorial body. The flow is linear, top-to-bottom: first a concise page controller block (page title + high-level controls), followed by a compact filter bar, then a vertically stacked scholarship list. A single-line support CTA appears as a distinct, standalone line toward the lower third of the content column, and the pagination sits at the very bottom. There are no sidebars; all interactive elements stay within this central column to preserve the focused, letter-like reading experience.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The top ~20–25% of the viewport is still occupied by the global header and navigation stack. The main content area beneath uses a centered column that spans roughly 40–50% of the viewport width on desktop, with large, empty left/right gutters occupying the remaining 50–60%. A generous vertical gap (~56–64px) separates the bottom of the navigation from the first line of the page controller, reinforcing a framed, intentional content area. Within the column, section blocks (page controller, filter bar, list, CTA, pagination) are separated by 24–40px vertical spacing, with slightly larger spacing (~40–48px) above the first block and above the support CTA to mark it as a focal element.
layout_strategies.content_density: The density remains low and editorial. Each component is kept visually light: filters are presented in a single concise row, list items are mostly text with clear line-height and only minimal separators, and pagination is compact and centered. Horizontal padding inside interactive elements follows the 16px pattern; vertical spacing between scholarship entries is around 24–32px. There are no heavy card backgrounds or busy multi-column layouts; everything feels like structured text and simple controls laid out on a clean white page.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The user’s eye moves down from the bright red utility bar and black navigation to the centered page controller heading, which replaces the ‘Dear Alumni’ role as the primary anchor. From there, the eye naturally drops to the filter bar directly beneath it, then into the vertical rhythm of scholarship entries. Around the point where scrolling begins to feel established, the standalone Scholarships Support CTA line appears slightly isolated with extra top margin, mirroring the LinkedIn line focal treatment from the mockup. Finally, the eye completes the journey at the bottom-centered pagination controls, which signal the end of the current content segment and the option to move to other pages.

layout.overall_description: Below the institutional red utility bar and black main navigation, the page opens into a broad white canvas. Centered on this canvas is a relatively narrow text column, about 40–50% of the viewport width, leaving wide, quiet gutters on both sides. After a generous vertical gap from the navigation, the Scholarships & Support page title and any high-level controls appear as a compact, editorial block at the top of this column, visually echoing the opening salutation in a formal letter. Immediately below, a single-row filter bar spans the same column width, offering straightforward controls without breaking the minimal, flat aesthetic. The main body of the page is a vertical stack of scholarship entries, each separated by ample whitespace and clear line-height, making the list feel more like a set of well-spaced paragraphs than dense cards. As the user scrolls, a single, centered support call-to-action line appears in the middle-to-lower part of the column with slightly more surrounding whitespace, creating a momentary focal pause similar to the standalone LinkedIn link in the mockup. At the bottom of the column, pagination controls are centered and compact, acting as a clean visual endpoint. Throughout, the layout is strictly linear and single-column, with horizontal bands only at the very top for the header and navigation, preserving the conservative, editorial character of the original design.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The page controller sits at the top of the main content column, directly below the global header/navigation stack, with a generous top margin of roughly 56–64px from the bottom edge of the black nav bar. It is centered horizontally, constrained to about 40–50% of the viewport width, with equal, wide gutters on the left and right. Within this block, the page title (e.g., “Scholarships & Support”) appears as the first line, left-aligned within the column, followed by any brief descriptive text or a compact sort/dropdown aligned to the right of the same column or just beneath the title with 16–24px vertical spacing. The overall height of this block is modest, occupying roughly 10–15% of the viewport height at most, preserving the feeling of a concise editorial header rather than a heavy hero.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Participates in normal document flow; not sticky or fixed. Always appears as the first element of the content column following the header/navigation.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The scholarship filter bar is placed immediately below the page controller, separated by a vertical gap of about 24–32px. It aligns perfectly with the same centered content column, matching its width (40–50% of viewport) and left/right edges. The filters are arranged in a single horizontal row where possible: key dropdowns or pill-style toggles are left-aligned within the column, with optional secondary controls (e.g., clear filters) aligned to the right edge of this same row, maintaining clean left and right anchors. Vertical padding inside the bar is modest (8–12px top and bottom), keeping the bar visually light; there is no full-width background band across the screen—only the controls themselves on white, reinforcing the minimal, editorial feel. The bar’s overall height is compact, roughly equivalent to one-and-a-half lines of text, so it reads as a functional accent between the title and the list rather than a dominant module.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows with the content. Optionally may become sticky within the main content area when scrolling (sticking just below the global navigation) but remains confined to the centered column width rather than stretching full-page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The scholarship list begins directly below the filter bar with a vertical gap of about 24–32px, still aligned to the same centered column width. Each scholarship entry spans the full width of this column, left-aligned, and is composed mainly of text: title, key details (e.g., eligibility, amount), and a subtle link or button for ‘View Details’ on the right or at the bottom of the entry. Entries are separated purely by whitespace and/or a very light bottom rule; vertical spacing between entries is generous, around 24–32px, so the list feels like a sequence of short, editorial sections rather than dense tiles. Visually, the list occupies the majority of the viewport height aside from the top header region, potentially extending to multiple scroll lengths, but it always maintains the narrow column and large side gutters to keep focus and reduce cognitive load.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard scrollable content in the main document flow. The list extends vertically as needed with no fixed height; entries reflow responsively while preserving the central column alignment.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The scholarship support CTA is placed within the same content column but treated as a standalone, highly legible line, echoing the LinkedIn URL treatment from the mockup. It appears after a group of scholarship entries—typically around the transition between the first and second screenful of results—or just above the pagination if the list is short. There is a slightly larger top margin (about 40–48px) separating it from the preceding scholarship entry, and a smaller bottom margin (24–32px) before the next element, creating a subtle visual pause. The CTA text is centered horizontally within the column or left-aligned but kept on a single line if possible, with any link styled in a distinct brand color (e.g., red or blue) to stand out against the black body text. The component itself is visually minimal: no large buttons or blocks, just a clear line of text on white, making it feel like a deliberate editorial callout.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Sits in normal document flow; not sticky. It should remain fixed in its relative position between list content and pagination (or mid-list), acting as a focal interruption in the vertical reading flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The pagination controls are anchored at the bottom of the main content column, following either the last scholarship entry or the support CTA, with a vertical gap of about 32–40px above them to clearly indicate the end of the current list. The controls are horizontally centered within the same column width so that page numbers, previous/next arrows, and any page size selector form a compact, centered cluster. Vertical padding around the clickable elements is modest (about 8px top and bottom), with 16px horizontal spacing between page numbers, keeping them readable yet restrained. This block occupies a small band at the bottom of the viewport once reached—roughly the height of two lines of text—reinforcing its role as a terminus for the current content segment.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Participates in normal document flow; appears only once the user scrolls to the bottom of the list. It is not fixed or floating, preserving the linear, top-to-bottom scrolling experience from the mockup.

name: Scholarship Detail
title: Scholarship Detail
description: Displays detailed information about a specific scholarship, allows users to save or unsave it to their personal list, add or edit a personal note, and navigate to related pages including giving and saved scholarships.

core_features: Load detailed scholarship data using getScholarshipDetail based on the scholarshipId URL parameter.
Display full scholarship details including description, eligibility group, level of study, eligibility details, award range, application requirements, and deadline.
Indicate applicable audiences by showing eligibility_group and level_of_study values (e.g., children_of_alumni, undergraduate) in a structured eligibility summary.
Provide a save/unsave control that uses saveScholarship and unsaveScholarship to add or remove the scholarship from the user’s Saved Scholarships list.
Provide a note field bound to the saved scholarship that uses saveScholarship (for first-time save with note) and updateSavedScholarshipNote (for subsequent edits) to persist a personalized note.

Show navigation controls: a Back to results link using the optional return_to URL param or a default Back to Scholarships link, and a View My Saved Scholarships link to my_saved.html with section=scholarships.
Optionally display a giving call-to-action that links to give.html with a fund_id associated with this scholarship when that mapping is provided by the surrounding system.
user_workflows: View scholarship details from a listing: 1) User clicks a scholarship on scholarships.html or my_saved.html; 2) Browser navigates to scholarship_detail.html with scholarshipId (and optional return_to); 3) On load, the page calls getScholarshipDetail(scholarshipId); 4) The page displays the scholarship details, eligibility summary, award and deadline, and application information; 5) The save status (saved/unsaved) and existing note (if any) are shown.

Save a new scholarship and add a note: 1) User lands on scholarship_detail.html for a scholarship not yet saved (is_saved=false); 2) User types a personal note into the note field; 3) User clicks Save Note or Save Scholarship; 4) The page calls saveScholarship(scholarshipId, note) with the current note value; 5) On success, the page updates is_saved=true, stores saved_scholarship_id from the response, and updates the displayed note; 6) The save control now appears as an Unsave action.

Add or edit a note for an already-saved scholarship: 1) User opens scholarship_detail.html for a scholarship where getScholarshipDetail reports is_saved=true and returns saved_note and saved_scholarship_id; 2) The note field is pre-populated with saved_note; 3) User modifies the note content; 4) User clicks Save Note; 5) The page calls updateSavedScholarshipNote(savedScholarshipId, note) using saved_scholarship_id from state; 6) On success, the displayed note and any status messages are updated.

Unsave a scholarship: 1) User is on scholarship_detail.html where the scholarship is currently saved (is_saved=true); 2) User clicks the Unsave button; 3) The page calls unsaveScholarship(scholarshipId); 4) On success, the page sets is_saved=false, clears saved_scholarship_id and saved_note in state, and visually updates the control to a Save option; 5) The note field is cleared or marked as inactive according to the page’s note-handling rules.

Navigate back to search results or listing: 1) User arrives with an optional return_to URL parameter representing the originating results context; 2) The Back to results link is rendered using return_to if present; 3) When the user clicks Back to results, the browser navigates to the decoded return_to URL; 4) If return_to is absent, a Back to Scholarships link navigates to scholarships.html without additional parameters.

Navigate to My Saved Scholarships: 1) From scholarship_detail.html, user clicks View My Saved Scholarships; 2) Browser navigates to my_saved.html?section=scholarships so that the My Saved page can focus on the scholarships section.

Follow giving call-to-action: 1) When an associated fund_id is available from the surrounding configuration for this scholarship, the page renders a giving CTA; 2) User clicks the giving CTA (e.g., Support Student Scholarships); 3) Browser navigates to give.html with fund_id set to the associated value (e.g., give.html?fund_id={fundIdForThisScholarship}).
interactions: On page load: Parse scholarshipId and optional return_to from the URL query string; if scholarshipId is present, call getScholarshipDetail(scholarshipId); handle loading and error states until a response is received.

On successful getScholarshipDetail response: Bind the scholarship object to detail components; set local state flags is_saved and saved_scholarship_id; populate the note editor with saved_note when is_saved is true; adjust save/unsave button label and behavior based on is_saved.

On Save Scholarship or Save Note click when the scholarship is not yet saved (is_saved=false): Read the current note field value; call saveScholarship(scholarshipId, note); on success, update is_saved=true, saved_scholarship_id to the returned SavedScholarship.id, and note to SavedScholarship.note; on failure, show an error state without altering existing state.

On Save Note click when the scholarship is already saved (is_saved=true): Read the current note field value; call updateSavedScholarshipNote(savedScholarshipId, note); on success, update the saved note in state to the returned SavedScholarship.note; on failure, keep the previous note in state and show an error message.

On Unsave click when is_saved=true: Call unsaveScholarship(scholarshipId); on success, set is_saved=false, clear saved_scholarship_id and note from state; on failure, leave state unchanged and show an error.
On note field change: Update the in-memory note value bound to the note editor component; do not call APIs until the user explicitly clicks Save Note (or Save Scholarship for first-time save).
On Back to results click: If return_to is available, navigate the browser to the decoded return_to value; otherwise navigate to scholarships.html.
On View My Saved Scholarships click: Navigate to my_saved.html?section=scholarships.
On giving CTA click (when present): Navigate to give.html with the associated fund_id as a URL parameter (e.g., give.html?fund_id={fundIdForThisScholarship}).

On application link click (when application_url is provided on the scholarship): Open application_url in the current tab or a new tab, as defined by site-wide behavior, without modifying any saved scholarship state.
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary external state is controlled via URL parameters and API responses. The scholarshipId URL parameter is required for the page to function; if it is missing, the page should not call getScholarshipDetail and instead show an error or redirect based on global behavior. On load, scholarship_detail.html reads scholarshipId and optional return_to from the query string. Using scholarshipId, the page calls getScholarshipDetail and stores in-memory state: scholarship (Scholarship object), is_saved (boolean), saved_scholarship_id (string or null), and current_note (initially saved_note from the response if is_saved is true, otherwise empty). All subsequent save, unsave, and note updates operate on this in-memory state and the assigned interfaces; no localStorage or other client-side persistence is used. After saveScholarship or updateSavedScholarshipNote completes successfully, the in-memory state is updated to reflect the SavedScholarship returned by the API. After unsaveScholarship completes successfully, the page clears the saved-related state. The return_to URL parameter, when present, is treated as an opaque token or URL value and is not modified; it is only used to construct the Back to results link. Navigation to my_saved.html always appends section=scholarships to the URL query string to ensure the target page can activate the scholarships section. Any fund_id used for the giving CTA is passed only through navigation (e.g., as a query string parameter to give.html) and is not stored or manipulated by this page beyond constructing the outbound URL.

components.functionality: Parses scholarshipId and optional return_to from the URL, calls getScholarshipDetail to retrieve the scholarship details and save status, and initializes page-level state used by other components.
Displays the scholarship name and key summary information such as eligibility group and level of study, and may include a visual indicator when the scholarship is saved.

Shows structured eligibility information based on eligibility_group, level_of_study, and eligibility_details so users can quickly determine whether the scholarship applies to groups such as children_of_alumni and specific study levels such as undergraduate.

Displays award information (minimum and optional maximum award_amount, award_amount_max, award_currency) and the scholarship deadline, and can indicate whether the scholarship is currently active based on is_active and deadline.

Shows application requirements and provides an Apply button or link when application_url is available, allowing the user to navigate to the external application while remaining independent of any save/note state.

Provides a Save/Unsave button that reflects whether the scholarship is currently in the user’s Saved Scholarships list. When unsaved, clicking the control will save the scholarship with the current note value if provided; when saved, clicking will remove it from the saved list.

Displays and edits the user’s personal note associated with the scholarship. If the scholarship is not yet saved, clicking Save Note triggers saveScholarship with the note; if already saved, clicking Save Note triggers updateSavedScholarshipNote with the saved_scholarship_id.
Shows success or error messages resulting from calls to saveScholarship, unsaveScholarship, and updateSavedScholarshipNote so users receive feedback on their actions.
Renders Back to results or Back to Scholarships based on the presence of return_to, and a View My Saved Scholarships link that routes to my_saved.html with section=scholarships.

When a fund_id association is available for this scholarship, displays a giving call-to-action that links to give.html with fund_id set appropriately, enabling users to support scholarships related to this opportunity.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column desktop grid with a full-width header and navigation, and a centered content column spanning approximately 6–8 columns with large side gutters.
layout.layout_pattern: All scholarship detail components sit inside a single, centered content column that reads like a formal letter. Above, the existing red utility bar and black navigation bar occupy the full width. Below them, a generous empty white space leads into the scholarship detail header as the first line of content. Every other component is stacked vertically in a linear top-to-bottom flow within the same narrow column: header, eligibility, award/deadline, application details, a mid-flow giving CTA, save controls and note editor, followed by navigation links at the bottom.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: All scholarship detail components sit inside a single, centered content column that reads like a formal letter. Above, the existing red utility bar and black navigation bar occupy the full width. Below them, a generous empty white space leads into the scholarship detail header as the first line of content. Every other component is stacked vertically in a linear top-to-bottom flow within the same narrow column: header, eligibility, award/deadline, application details, a mid-flow giving CTA, save controls and note editor, followed by navigation links at the bottom.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The global header and navigation retain roughly the top 20–25% of the viewport. From their bottom edge, there is a 56–64px gap before the first line of scholarship content. The content column itself occupies about 35–45% of the viewport width on desktop (approximately 6–8 of 12 grid columns), centered with large white gutters on both sides. Vertical spacing between the stacked scholarship sections follows the 8px base unit: 32–40px between closely related blocks (e.g., header and eligibility), 48–64px before more conceptually distinct sections (e.g., before note editor and bottom navigation links).
layout_strategies.content_density: The content density is low and editorial: no sidebars, cards, or heavy chrome. Text blocks are short, with 16–24px paragraph spacing and generous line height. Functional elements (save toggle, note editor, navigation links) are visually light and integrated into the letter-like flow, avoiding dense clusters. Information is divided into discrete, well-spaced sections that can be scanned vertically without distraction.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The eye moves from the bright red utility bar at the top, down through the black navigation bar, then drops into a calm white space that frames the scholarship detail header as the first focal point. From there, the reader follows the scholarship information in a straight vertical sequence: title and essentials (eligibility, award, application), then a standalone giving CTA line that echoes the LinkedIn link moment from the mockup, then personal interaction elements (save, note, status feedback), and finally the navigation links at the bottom as a soft exit path from the page.

layout.overall_description: Within the existing red utility header and black navigation bar, the Scholarship Detail page opens into a large, quiet white canvas. Centered on this canvas is a relatively narrow text column—about two-fifths of the viewport wide—that feels like a formal letter printed on a sheet of paper. After a substantial blank band of white space below the navigation, the scholarship name appears at the top of this column, aligned left within the narrow text area but visually anchored in the center of the screen. Underneath, key scholarship details unfold as short, well-spaced paragraphs and sections—eligibility, award amount and deadlines, and application instructions—each separated by consistent vertical gaps. Midway, a single giving CTA line stands out on its own, like a highlighted URL in a letter. Further down, small, unobtrusive controls for saving the scholarship and editing a personal note sit within the same column, followed by brief inline status messages when actions occur. At the very bottom, a set of simple text links guide users to related destinations like giving and saved scholarships. The entire page is calm, linear, and typographic, with all attention focused on the central column while the broad left and right gutters remain empty, reinforcing the institutional, editorial character of the design.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The data loader appears as a subtle overlay centered within the white content canvas, directly over the narrow central column. It does not disturb the red utility bar or black navigation bar; instead, it occupies the area starting roughly 56–64px below the navigation, extending down just enough to cover the typical vertical zone of the scholarship content. A simple spinner or loading indicator is visually centered horizontally on the page and vertically within this visible white area, with ample white space around it. The width of the loader aligns to the content column (about 35–45% of the viewport), so the perception is of a single sheet of paper temporarily showing a loading state instead of text.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Appears as a centered overlay constrained to the main content region beneath the global navigation; it temporarily sits above other scholarship detail components and disappears when data is loaded.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The scholarship detail header is the first visible content element beneath the global navigation. It sits approximately 56–64px below the bottom edge of the black navigation bar, creating a deliberate band of empty white space. The header content is placed within a centered column that spans roughly 40% of the viewport width (6–8 of 12 columns), with large equal gutters on left and right. The scholarship title appears at the top of this column, left-aligned within the column but visually centered on the page due to the symmetrical gutters. A short summary line or subtitle, if present, sits 8–16px below the title. Vertical padding above and below the header block is modest but consistent (around 24–32px total), so it feels like the salutation of a formal letter.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static element in the normal document flow, anchoring the top of the content column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The eligibility summary appears directly beneath the header within the same centered text column. There is a vertical gap of about 24–32px from the bottom of the header to the first line of the eligibility block, following the editorial spacing rhythm. A brief section label (e.g., “Eligibility”) may appear as a subtle, slightly bolder line at the top of this block, with the actual eligibility text expressed as one or two short paragraphs beneath it. All text is left-aligned within the column, with 16–24px spacing between paragraphs, and there are no borders or background boxes—just clean text on white. The block’s overall width is identical to the header’s, maintaining a single continuous column.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, stacked immediately below the header as part of the linear reading path.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The award and deadline section follows the eligibility summary as another clearly separated text block within the same central column. A vertical space of about 32px (four base units) is left between the eligibility text and the first line of this section, signaling a new cluster of information while keeping the flow continuous. The block begins with a concise heading or label (e.g., “Award & Deadlines”) followed by short paragraphs or line items describing the award amount, number of awards, and key dates. Content is left-aligned, respecting the same column width and typographic rhythm, with 16–24px between paragraphs or lines. There are no side-by-side columns; if multiple details are listed, they appear stacked vertically to preserve the letter-like aesthetic.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, occupies its place in the vertical stack and expands vertically as needed without affecting the centered width.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The application section is positioned just below the award/deadline block, separated by another 32–40px vertical gap. It continues within the same centered text column, left-aligned, and may include a short section label (e.g., “How to Apply”) with one or more concise paragraphs explaining the application process, required documents, and any key links. If an external application URL is present, it is highlighted as an inline or standalone line, but remains consistent with the sober, textual style (e.g., underlined or brand-colored text rather than a large button). Paragraphs and bullet-like instructions are arranged vertically, with generous line height and 16–24px between distinct paragraphs, keeping the section airy and easy to scan.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, forming the next segment in the linear reading sequence, stretching vertically as content demands while maintaining the fixed column width.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The giving CTA is placed as a distinct focal line of text between the main scholarship information and the more personal interaction elements. It sits roughly 32–40px below the application section, still within the same centered column, but given its own breathing room: additional 16–24px of white space above and below the CTA text so it feels like a standalone sentence in the middle of the page. The CTA itself appears as a single line or very short phrase (e.g., “Learn about supporting scholarships”) styled as a link in a brand color, echoing the mid-body LinkedIn URL from the mockup. It remains left-aligned within the column but visually prominent due to its isolation and color, creating a clear moment in the reading flow without introducing heavy button styles.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the column, occupying a single-line-height band with extended vertical padding to emphasize its standalone character.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The save controls appear slightly below the giving CTA, separated by about 32px of vertical space, and remain within the same centered text column. To respect the editorial feel while still making the control easy to find, the save toggle or button is aligned to the right edge of the content column, so it sits visually in the upper-right corner of this central ‘page’ area but does not extend into the gutters. It may be accompanied by a short label (e.g., “Save this scholarship”) placed immediately to its left or above it with 8px spacing. Vertical padding around the control is limited (around 16px top and bottom), so it reads as a discrete but unobtrusive utility element before the more text-heavy note editor below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, right-aligned within the central column; remains in normal document flow and does not stick or float on scroll.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The note editor sits below the save controls, separated by a comfortable 24–32px gap, acting like the ‘personal notes’ area at the end of a formal letter. It occupies the full width of the centered column. A simple label such as “Your Note” appears left-aligned at the top of this block, with 8–12px of space above the note input area. The input itself is a multi-line textarea with minimal styling: a thin, neutral border and 8–12px internal padding, no heavy shadows or colored backgrounds, and a height that suggests 3–6 lines of text by default. The block as a whole has modest vertical padding (around 24px total above and below), but still maintains generous white space beneath it before bottom navigation links, preserving the low-density feel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the vertical flow, expanding vertically if the textarea grows but always constrained to the central column width.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Status messages are displayed inline, close to the interactions they relate to, but still within the same central column and respecting the editorial typography. When feedback relates to saving the scholarship, the message appears directly beneath the save controls, with a small 8–12px gap separating it. When related to notes, it appears just below the note editor label or at the bottom of the note editor block, separated by 8–12px. The messages use a single short line of text in a slightly emphasized color (e.g., green for success, red for error) but maintain the same font size and left alignment as the body text. They do not show as large banners; instead, they sit inside the text flow as a subtle annotation.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Ephemeral inline elements that appear and disappear within the central column without shifting the layout structure beyond their own line height.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The navigation links are placed near the bottom of the content column, below the note editor and any associated status messages, with a more generous vertical margin of about 40–48px to indicate a shift from content to onward paths. Within the same centered column width, these links are arranged as a short vertical list of text items (e.g., “Back to Scholarships,” “View Saved Scholarships”), each on its own line with 8–12px vertical spacing between them. They are left-aligned, styled as standard text links (subtle underline or color) without icons or buttons. The block as a whole feels like the closing section of the letter, giving gentle, low-key options for what to do next.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static at the bottom of the main content stack, expanding vertically as links are added but always constrained to the central column.

name: My Saved Items & Contacts
title: My Saved Items and Contacts
description: Page where users can review and manage their saved benefits, scholarships, and alumni contacts, including editing personal notes and tags and navigating back to related detail pages.

core_features: Load all saved benefits, scholarships, and contact lists for the current user using getSavedItemsOverview on page load.

Segment the page into three main sections—Saved Benefits, Saved Scholarships, and Contacts—and focus the initial section based on the URL parameter `section` (values: "benefits", "scholarships", "contacts").
Display a list of saved benefits (SavedBenefit + Benefit) with links to benefit_detail.html and controls to edit notes and remove items via updateSavedBenefitNote and unsaveBenefit.

Display a list of saved scholarships (SavedScholarship + Scholarship) with links to scholarship_detail.html and controls to edit notes and remove items via updateSavedScholarshipNote and unsaveScholarship.

Display one or more contact lists, each with its entries (ContactListEntry + AlumniProfile), and controls to edit notes and tags via updateContactListEntry and remove entries via removeContactListEntry.
Provide navigation links from each section to the related discovery pages (benefits.html, scholarships.html, alumni_directory.html).
Handle empty states per section (no saved benefits, no saved scholarships, no contacts) by showing an informational message and a link to the relevant discovery page.
Provide inline feedback for operations (saving notes, updating tags, removing items) using success and error messages returned by the update and remove interfaces.
user_workflows:
View all saved items and contacts:
1. User navigates to my_saved.html (optionally with ?section=benefits|scholarships|contacts).
2. On page load, the page container component reads the `section` URL parameter and determines which section to focus; if the parameter is missing or invalid, a default section is selected.
3. The page triggers getSavedItemsOverview with no parameters.
4. When the response returns, the page stores the returned saved_benefits, saved_scholarships, and contact_lists in page state.
5. The section switcher focuses the section defined by `section` and scrolls or sets focus to that section.
6. Each section component renders its list from the bound data models; empty sections render an empty-state message and a navigation link to the relevant exploration page.

Edit a note on a saved benefit:
1. In the Saved Benefits section, the user clicks an "Edit note" control on a specific saved benefit row.
2. The row enters an inline edit state, displaying a text input or textarea pre-populated from SavedBenefit.note.
3. The user modifies the note and clicks a "Save" control for that note.
4. The component triggers updateSavedBenefitNote with parameters: savedBenefitId (from SavedBenefit.id) and note (current input value).
5. While waiting for the response, the component may show a loading state for that note.
6. If the response.success is true, the component updates the local SavedBenefit.note with the returned saved_benefit.note and exits edit mode, showing a success message derived from response.message.
7. If success is false, the component keeps the edit mode open and displays an error message based on response.message.

Remove a saved benefit from the list:
1. In the Saved Benefits section, the user clicks a "Remove" or similar control for a specific saved benefit.
2. A confirmation dialog component is shown, referencing the selected SavedBenefit and Benefit (e.g., by name, using dynamic data).
3. If the user confirms, the component calls unsaveBenefit with benefitId from Benefit.id.
4. While waiting, the row or dialog shows a loading state.
5. If the response.success is true, the component removes the associated SavedBenefit item from the local saved_benefits array and closes the dialog, showing a success notification.
6. If success is false, the item remains in the list and an error notification is displayed using response.message.

Edit a note on a saved scholarship:
1. In the Saved Scholarships section, the user clicks an "Edit note" control on a saved scholarship row.
2. The row enters inline edit mode, with input initialized from SavedScholarship.note.
3. The user modifies the note and clicks a "Save" control.
4. The component calls updateSavedScholarshipNote with parameters: savedScholarshipId (SavedScholarship.id) and note (current input value).
5. On success, the local SavedScholarship.note is replaced with the updated value from saved_scholarship.note, edit mode closes, and a success message is shown.
6. On failure, the edit mode stays open and an error message based on response.message is displayed.

Remove a saved scholarship from the list:
1. The user clicks a "Remove" control on a saved scholarship row.
2. A confirmation dialog appears.
3. On confirmation, the component calls unsaveScholarship with scholarshipId from Scholarship.id.
4. On success, the scholarship is removed from the local saved_scholarships list and a success notification is shown.
5. On failure, the item stays visible and an error notification is shown.

Update a contact note and tags:
1. In the Contacts section, within a specific contact list, the user clicks an "Edit" control for a contact entry.
2. The entry row reveals editable fields: a note field initialized from ContactListEntry.note and a tags input initialized from ContactListEntry.tags.
3. The user updates the note and/or tags and clicks "Save".
4. The component calls updateContactListEntry with parameters: contactListEntryId (ContactListEntry.id), note (current note value), tags (current tags array from the tags input).
5. On success, the local ContactListEntry is updated from contact_list_entry in the response, edit mode closes, and a success message is displayed using response.message.
6. On failure, the edit mode remains, and the error is shown without discarding the user’s unsaved changes unless the user cancels.

Remove an alumni contact from a contact list:
1. The user clicks a "Remove" or similar control on a contact entry.
2. A confirmation dialog appears.
3. On confirmation, the component calls removeContactListEntry with contactListEntryId from ContactListEntry.id.
4. On success, the entry is removed from the corresponding contact list’s entries array and a success notification is shown.
5. On failure, the entry remains and an error message is displayed.

Navigate from a saved item/contact to its detail page:
1. In any section, the user clicks a primary link (e.g., the benefit name, scholarship name, or contact name).
2. The page navigates to the appropriate detail page using URL parameters:
   - For benefits: benefit_detail.html?benefitId=Benefit.id
   - For scholarships: scholarship_detail.html?scholarshipId=Scholarship.id
   - For contacts: alumni_profile.html?alumniProfileId=AlumniProfile.id.
3. The target page uses the passed ID to load and display the selected record.

Arrive on the page focused on a specific section via `section` URL parameter:
1. The user clicks a "View my saved" link from another page such as benefit_detail.html, scholarship_detail.html, or alumni_profile.html; the link includes a section query parameter (e.g., my_saved.html?section=scholarships).
2. On load, the My Saved page reads the `section` parameter.
3. If the value matches one of the expected sections, the corresponding section’s tab is activated and its content is prioritized in viewport focus.
4. If the parameter is missing or invalid, the default section is activated (for example, benefits).
5. The user can switch sections using the section switcher, which updates the `section` parameter in the URL without requiring new data from getSavedItemsOverview.
interactions: Click on section tabs or navigation controls to switch between Saved Benefits, Saved Scholarships, and Contacts; this updates the URL `section` parameter and the visible section without re-calling getSavedItemsOverview.
Click on a saved benefit name or explicit "View benefit" link to navigate to benefit_detail.html with benefitId populated from the Benefit.id field.
Click on a saved scholarship name or explicit "View scholarship" link to navigate to scholarship_detail.html with scholarshipId populated from Scholarship.id.
Click on a contact name or "View profile" link to navigate to alumni_profile.html with alumniProfileId populated from AlumniProfile.id.
Click on section-level "Explore more benefits" link to navigate to benefits.html without additional parameters.
Click on section-level "Explore more scholarships" link to navigate to scholarships.html without additional parameters.
Click on section-level "Find more alumni" link to navigate to alumni_directory.html without additional parameters.
Click on "Edit note" in a saved benefit row to toggle that row into note edit mode; input changes are tracked in local component state until submission.
Click on "Save" within a benefit note editor to trigger updateSavedBenefitNote; submit is disabled while the request is in flight to prevent duplicate submissions.
Click on "Cancel" within a benefit note editor to exit edit mode and revert the input to the last saved note value.
Click on "Remove" in a benefit row to open a confirmation dialog; confirming triggers unsaveBenefit and, on success, removes the item from the list.
Click on "Edit note" in a saved scholarship row to enable inline note editing; saving triggers updateSavedScholarshipNote and cancel reverts changes.
Click on "Remove" in a scholarship row to open a confirmation dialog; confirming triggers unsaveScholarship and, on success, removes the item from the list.
Click on a contact entry’s "Edit" action to expand note and tags editing controls; tags can be added, removed, or renamed within local component state before saving.
Click on "Save" in a contact entry editor to trigger updateContactListEntry with the current note and tags; on success, the row reflects updated data and collapses the editor.
Click on "Remove" in a contact entry to open a confirmation dialog; confirming triggers removeContactListEntry and, on success, removes the entry from its contact list.
Expand or collapse a contact list (if multiple lists exist) by clicking the list header; expansion state is managed on the client and does not affect URL parameters.
Observe notification banners or inline messages that appear after update or remove operations; these messages derive their content from the `message` field in interface responses.
page_functionality.state_logic: On page load, the page reads the URL query parameter `section`. If `section` equals "benefits", "scholarships", or "contacts", that value becomes the initial active section; otherwise a default section is selected (for example, "benefits"). The active section state is mirrored in the UI via the section switcher and controls which section is scrolled into view. When the user switches sections via the section switcher, the page updates the URL (e.g., my_saved.html?section=scholarships) using standard navigation or history APIs so that the current section is always encoded in the URL; this enables deep-linking back to the same section without requiring additional API calls. The underlying data for all sections is loaded once per page load via getSavedItemsOverview and kept in memory; switching sections does not re-fetch data. Editing notes or tags and removing items update the in-memory collections directly in response to successful interface calls (updateSavedBenefitNote, updateSavedScholarshipNote, updateContactListEntry, unsaveBenefit, unsaveScholarship, removeContactListEntry), ensuring the UI stays in sync with server state. No persistent client storage such as localStorage is used; any state beyond what is encoded in the URL (the active section) is transient and reset on full page reload.

components.functionality: Initializes the My Saved Items and Contacts page, reads the `section` URL parameter, calls getSavedItemsOverview, stores the returned data, and passes it to child components along with the active section.
Displays controls to switch between Saved Benefits, Saved Scholarships, and Contacts; updates the URL `section` parameter and notifies the page container of the active section.

Renders the Saved Benefits section when active, including a list of saved benefits, inline note editing for each item, removal controls, navigation links to benefit_detail.html, and a section-level link to benefits.html.
Provides inline editing for an individual SavedBenefit.note field, handling user input, submission via updateSavedBenefitNote, and displaying success or error feedback.

Renders the Saved Scholarships section when active, including a list of saved scholarships, inline note editing, removal controls, navigation links to scholarship_detail.html, and a section-level link to scholarships.html.
Handles inline editing of SavedScholarship.note values for individual saved scholarships, calling updateSavedScholarshipNote on save and showing error or success states based on the response.

Displays all available contact lists and their entries when the Contacts section is active, including expand/collapse behavior for each contact list, inline editing of notes and tags, removal of entries, navigation to alumni_profile.html, and a section-level link to alumni_directory.html.
Provides editable controls for a single ContactListEntry, including note and tags fields, and calls updateContactListEntry with updated values on save.
Shows an empty-state message and a relevant navigation link when a specific section (benefits, scholarships, or contacts) has no items to display based on the getSavedItemsOverview response.
Presents a confirmation step before destructive actions such as unsaveBenefit, unsaveScholarship, and removeContactListEntry; routes the user’s confirmation to the appropriate interface call.

Displays transient success or error notifications after note updates or item removals, using the `message` property returned from updateSavedBenefitNote, updateSavedScholarshipNote, updateContactListEntry, unsaveBenefit, unsaveScholarship, and removeContactListEntry.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column desktop grid with a full-width header and navigation, and a centered content column spanning approximately 6–8 columns with large side gutters.
layout.layout_pattern: Below the existing global utility header and main navigation, this page uses a single, centered content column that behaves like a formal, editorial body. All components—section switcher, lists, inline editors, empty states, and notifications—are stacked vertically within this column in a linear top-to-bottom flow. Only one content section (benefits, scholarships, or contacts) is visually dominant at a time, controlled by the section tabs near the top of the content area.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Below the existing global utility header and main navigation, this page uses a single, centered content column that behaves like a formal, editorial body. All components—section switcher, lists, inline editors, empty states, and notifications—are stacked vertically within this column in a linear top-to-bottom flow. Only one content section (benefits, scholarships, or contacts) is visually dominant at a time, controlled by the section tabs near the top of the content area.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The global red utility bar, black navigation bar, and any site-wide sub-nav continue to occupy the top ~20–25% of the viewport. The remaining ~75–80% is a white canvas. The main content sits in a centered column spanning roughly 35–45% of the viewport width (about 6–8 columns of a 12-column grid), with large, empty left and right gutters. The section switcher sits near the top of this column, followed by the active list section and inline editors. Vertical spacing follows the mockup: a large 48–64px gap between navigation and the page’s first interactive element, 24–32px between major components, and 16–24px between related elements like list items and inline note editors.
layout_strategies.content_density: The layout maintains low content density: few elements per vertical slice, generous white space, and no sidebars. Lists are presented as simple, text-first rows with minimal decoration and clear breathing room around them. Inline editors expand within the same narrow column without introducing visual clutter, and notifications are slim bars or short text blocks that do not crowd the content. Each component is allowed ample space so users can scan saved items and notes without cognitive overload.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The user’s eye moves top-to-bottom: first the bright utility bar and dark navigation (already defined by the global layout), then any temporary notification banner immediately below them, then a comfortable white gap leading into the centered section switcher. From there, the active tab subtly anchors attention, leading into the corresponding list section. Within each section, users scan down the list of saved items; when an inline editor is opened, the eye drops slightly to an indented editor area directly beneath the item. When triggered, the confirmation dialog interrupts this flow by appearing centered over a dimmed background. The overall flow remains linear and calm, with only the modal creating a deliberate focal interruption when necessary.

layout.overall_description: Within the familiar institutional frame of the site’s red utility bar and black main navigation, the “My Saved Items & Contacts” page reads like a structured but minimal document. Below the nav, a generous band of white space opens the page, leading into a narrow, centered content column that feels like the body of a formal letter on a clean sheet. At the top of this column, a simple horizontal section switcher lets users choose between Saved Benefits, Saved Scholarships, and Contacts without breaking the quiet, editorial tone. Whichever section is active fills the center column with a vertical list of text-first entries, each separated by modest white space and aligned neatly down the page. When a user adds or edits notes or tags, an inline editor appears directly beneath the relevant entry, slightly indented but still within the same column, preserving the single-column reading rhythm. Success or error notifications slide in as a slim band just under the main navigation, spanning the same full width as the header but not disrupting the central column. If a user performs a destructive action, a centered modal dialog with a dimmed backdrop appears over the entire viewport, briefly overriding the calm flow to demand a clear decision, then vanishing to restore the simple, linear reading experience.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The page container begins directly below the existing global navigation and any site-wide sub-nav row. After a tall vertical gap of approximately 56–64px of empty white space, it defines a centered content column that spans roughly 40% of the viewport width (about 6–8 columns in a 12-column grid), with equal left and right gutters occupying the remaining 60%. All subsequent components on this page—section switcher, list sections, editors, and conditional messages—are vertically stacked inside this column, aligned flush left with a ragged right edge, resembling the typesetting of a formal letter. The container maintains consistent side padding of around 24–32px within the column, ensuring that text never touches the column’s edge. It extends downward with natural document-like scroll, without any side panels or fixed elements inside it.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block-level container in the normal document flow, centered horizontally with a max-width constraint and responsive gutters that widen on larger screens and reduce on smaller ones while preserving a single-column layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The section switcher is positioned at the top of the my-saved-page-container, appearing as the first interactive element inside the centered column after the initial 56–64px white gap below navigation. It spans nearly the full width of the content column (about 90–100% of the column width), aligned to the left edge of the column, and presents a short horizontal row of 2–3 text-based tabs (e.g., Benefits, Scholarships, Contacts). Each tab is rendered as plain text with 16px horizontal padding and 8–12px vertical padding, sitting on the white background, with the active tab underlined by a thin brand-colored rule that mirrors the subtle navigation underline in the mockup. Vertical space of roughly 24–32px separates the tab row from the list section below, giving the switcher a distinct but understated presence. There are no heavy borders or pills; the overall feel is flat, conservative, and aligned with the editorial style.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static horizontal control anchored to the top of the content column; it scrolls away with the page content and does not behave as sticky or floating.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The saved benefits section appears directly below the section switcher when the corresponding tab is active. It begins after a vertical gap of approximately 24–32px, maintaining the gentle separation from the tabs. The section occupies the full width of the content column (about 35–45% of viewport width), with all benefit entries left-aligned in a single vertical stack. Each saved benefit is represented as a concise text block: a title line, optional short metadata line (e.g., category or provider), and actions like 'View details' or 'Edit notes' aligned inline or on the right edge of the column, but still within the same text band. Each benefit item is separated from the next by 16–24px of vertical space, keeping the list airy. There are no card borders; the separation is created solely with spacing and consistent alignment. If inline editors are opened, they appear immediately beneath the relevant benefit entry, pushing subsequent items downward but remaining within the same narrow column. A bottom margin of around 32–40px below the last benefit visually closes the section before the next major component (or page end).
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static list section that appears or hides based on the active tab; it scrolls as part of the main document flow and expands vertically as more saved benefits are present.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The saved benefit note editor appears inline within the saved benefits section, directly beneath the specific benefit item being edited. It is indented slightly (around 8–16px) from the left edge of the main benefit text, creating a subtle nested hierarchy while still staying inside the content column. The editor spans roughly 90–100% of the column width and typically includes a multiline text area and a small set of controls such as 'Save' and 'Cancel' buttons. Vertical padding inside the editor follows the component padding rules—around 12–16px above and below the text area—framed by the white background without heavy borders; at most, a thin, light gray outline or top rule delineates it. A modest vertical gap of 8–12px separates the benefit’s text from the editor, and another 16–24px divides the bottom of the editor from the next list item, preserving the low-density, editorial feel even when editing is active.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline expanding region that appears in place within the list, pushing subsequent content downward. It does not overlay other content and scrolls naturally with the page; it is only visible for the currently edited benefit.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The saved scholarships section occupies the same spatial position and proportions as the saved benefits section but is only visible when its tab is active. It sits directly beneath the section switcher with the same 24–32px top margin and spans the full width of the centered content column. Each scholarship entry is rendered as a vertically stacked text block—title, short descriptive line or eligibility note, and a small link or button to 'View scholarship' and 'Edit notes.' Entries are left-aligned, with 16–24px vertical space between them, mirroring the spacing used in the benefits list to maintain consistency. There are no visual dividers or cards; instead, whitespace and alignment separate entries. The bottom of this section maintains a 32–40px margin before any following content or page footer, creating a clean visual endpoint when the user scrolls to the last saved scholarship.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static list section that replaces other sections when its tab is active; it expands vertically within the page container and scrolls in the normal document flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The saved scholarship note editor appears directly beneath the scholarship entry being edited, nested within the scholarships section. It aligns with the scholarship text block but is slightly indented (8–16px) to signal that it is a child element. The editor’s width follows the column, occupying roughly 90–100% of its width, and contains a multiline text input with minimal chrome, plus compact actions such as 'Save' and 'Cancel' arranged horizontally below or to the right of the text area. Internal padding respects the 12–16px vertical and 16px horizontal guidelines, giving the editor breathing room without looking like a separate card. A small vertical gap (8–12px) separates the scholarship text and the editor, and 16–24px separates the bottom of the editor from the next list item, so the overall list retains a calm, readable rhythm.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline expanding element that appears and disappears in place between list items. It pushes content below it downward and scrolls with the page, with no overlay or fixed behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contacts section occupies the same central column position as the benefits and scholarships sections and is shown when the Contacts tab is active. It begins beneath the section switcher with a consistent 24–32px vertical gap and fills the full width of the content column. Each contact entry is a simple text-first row: the contact’s name on the primary line, with a secondary line for role, graduating class, or organization, and subtle inline actions such as 'View profile,' 'Message,' or 'Edit notes' aligned either at the end of the primary line or on a separate action line. Entries are stacked vertically with 16–24px spacing between them, without boxes or heavy separators, echoing the letter-like layout. A slightly larger vertical gap (24–32px) can separate logical sub-groups (e.g., different organizations) if necessary, but overall the section remains a single, flowing column of contacts. The bottom of this section maintains about 32–40px of white space before the next page element or footer.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static vertical list that appears in place when its tab is active; it scrolls with the main page content and expands or contracts based on the number of contacts.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact entry editor appears inline beneath the specific contact row being edited. It is slightly indented relative to the main contact text (about 8–16px), visually indicating its subordinate relationship while still staying within the central column. The editor spans roughly the full width of the column and contains a multiline notes field and possibly small tag chips or a tag input aligned under the notes, with compact actions like 'Save' and 'Cancel' arranged immediately below or to the right. Internal spacing is modest but comfortable: 12–16px vertical padding around inputs and 16px horizontal padding. A small 8–12px gap separates the contact row from the editor, and 16–24px of space separates the bottom of the editor from the next contact, ensuring that even with multiple editors open the list retains an airy, organized feel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline expanding component that is inserted below the selected contact in the normal document flow. It scrolls with the page without floating, and only one or a limited number of editors are typically visible at once to maintain clarity.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The empty state message appears within the my-saved-page-container, occupying the same central column used by all other sections. When there are no saved items for the currently active tab (or for all tabs, depending on logic), the empty state replaces the corresponding list section. It is vertically centered within the viewport as much as possible while still respecting the global header—starting after the usual 56–64px top gap and then adding additional top margin (e.g., 64–96px) so the message sits visually in the middle of the white canvas. The message itself is a short, left-aligned text block in the column, possibly with a secondary explanation line beneath and a single subtle link or button (e.g., 'Explore benefits') placed on its own line. The block spans about 60–80% of the column width, leaving ample whitespace around it, and there are no illustrations or panels, keeping with the spare, document-like aesthetic.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditional static content that appears in place of list sections when there are no saved items. It scrolls as needed but is usually short enough to fit within a single viewport on desktop.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The confirmation dialog appears as a modal overlay centered both horizontally and vertically within the browser viewport, independent of the page’s scroll position. Behind it, the entire page—including the red utility bar, black navigation bar, and white content column—is dimmed with a semi-transparent dark scrim, subtly muting all other elements while still allowing users to recognize the site context. The dialog itself is a compact rectangle with a width of approximately 25–35% of the viewport, never exceeding the width of the central content column by much, reinforcing the conservative institutional look. Inside, content is arranged in a simple vertical stack: a short, bold title line, a concise explanatory sentence or two, and a row of primary and secondary action buttons right-aligned at the bottom. Padding inside the dialog follows the spacing system (16–24px on all sides), and there is no heavy decoration—just a clean, flat panel with perhaps a subtle border or drop shadow to lift it from the scrim.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed-position modal centered in the viewport with a full-screen scrim backdrop. It sits above all other page content, prevents interaction with underlying components while open, and remains fixed even if the user attempts to scroll.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The notification banner appears as a slim horizontal bar directly beneath the main navigation bar (and any global sub-nav), spanning the full page width from the left to right edge, visually continuing the stacked band pattern seen at the top of the mockup. It sits above the my-saved-page-container’s large top margin, effectively narrowing the gap slightly while a notification is present. The banner has a modest height of around 40–56px and uses a background tone that contrasts gently with the white content area (for example, a light-tinted success or warning color) while remaining less intense than the brand red header. Its content—a brief single-line message, optionally with a small icon and a 'Dismiss' or 'Undo' link—is centered within the same max-width as the header content, but the text itself is left-aligned within that frame, maintaining the editorial feel. Once the banner disappears, the page returns to its more spacious white gap between nav and content.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, temporary bar that appears in the normal flow between the global navigation and the page content. It pushes the content column downward while visible and scrolls away with the page; it does not remain sticky at the top when the user scrolls.

name: About the Alumni Office
title: About the Alumni Office
description: Page that loads and displays alumni office mission, services, contact information, and key policies using the getAlumniOfficeInfo interface, and routes users to core alumni site sections.

core_features: Call getAlumniOfficeInfo on page load to retrieve mission, services, contact_information, policies, and core_sections.
Display the alumni office mission text from the mission field returned by getAlumniOfficeInfo.
List alumni office services using the services array returned by getAlumniOfficeInfo, with each service’s title and description shown.
Show alumni office contact information using contact_information.email_addresses, contact_information.phone_numbers, and contact_information.mailing_address.
Present key policies (privacy, terms of use, accessibility) using the policies.privacy_summary, policies.terms_of_use_summary, and policies.accessibility_summary fields from getAlumniOfficeInfo.
Provide an in-page navigation to jump between sections (mission, services, contact, policies, core sections) based on anchors and the section URL parameter.

Use the core_sections array from getAlumniOfficeInfo to render links that navigate to core alumni site pages (events, giving, chapters, benefits, directory, mentoring, scholarships) based on each item’s code.
Offer a link within the services area that routes users to newsletter_subscribe.html with the source=about_page URL parameter for email newsletter sign-up.

Support deep-linking via the section URL parameter (e.g., about.html?section=privacy_terms) to automatically focus and scroll to the relevant section, such as the policies section for privacy and terms.
user_workflows:
Workflow: Read the alumni office mission
1. User navigates to about.html (no section parameter required).
2. The page initializes the about-office-data-loader component, which calls getAlumniOfficeInfo.
3. Once getAlumniOfficeInfo returns successfully, the mission-section component receives mission and renders the mission text.
4. The user can optionally click an in-page navigation item (e.g., “Mission”) which invokes mission-section as the scroll target and updates the URL to about.html?section=mission without reloading the page.

Workflow: Understand services and follow the newsletter link
1. User arrives on about.html.
2. getAlumniOfficeInfo returns services (array of objects with title and description).
3. The services-section component renders the list of services.
4. Within or adjacent to this list, an “Email newsletters” action link is shown (UI label only; not hardcoded content text from the API).
5. When the user clicks the “Email newsletters” link, the services-section component triggers navigation to newsletter_subscribe.html?source=about_page.
6. The user completes newsletter subscription on the dedicated newsletter_subscribe.html page (outside the scope of this page).

Workflow: Find and use contact information
1. User opens about.html.
2. After getAlumniOfficeInfo resolves, the contact-info-panel receives contact_information and displays email addresses, phone numbers, and mailing address.
3. The user clicks on an email address, triggering the default mail client using a mailto: link.
4. The user clicks on a phone number on a supported device, triggering the default tel: handler.
5. If the user uses in-page navigation and selects “Contact,” the URL updates to about.html?section=contact and the contact-info-panel is scrolled into view.

Workflow: View privacy, terms of use, and accessibility via deep link
1. User clicks “Privacy & Terms” in the global footer from any page (e.g., index.html), which navigates the browser to about.html?section=privacy_terms.
2. On page load, about-page-anchor-router parses section=privacy_terms.
3. about-office-data-loader calls getAlumniOfficeInfo and passes the policies data to the policies-section.
4. Once policies-section has rendered, about-page-anchor-router instructs it to expand or focus the privacy and terms-related content and scrolls to that part of the page.
5. The user can expand/collapse specific policy summaries (privacy, terms of use, accessibility) using the controls in policies-section; open/closed state is kept in memory only and not persisted outside the page.

Workflow: Navigate to a core alumni section from the About page
1. User lands on about.html (with or without a section parameter).
2. getAlumniOfficeInfo returns core_sections (each item includes code, title, and description).
3. core-sections-navigation uses the code field to map each item to a specific target page URL:
   - code="events" → events.html
   - code="make_a_gift" → give.html
   - code="chapters" → chapters.html
   - code="benefits" → benefits.html
   - code="directory" → alumni_directory.html
   - code="mentoring" → mentoring.html
   - code="scholarships" → scholarships.html
   (Any other codes can be handled according to a generic routing rule or ignored, based on implementation policy.)
4. The user clicks on a core section item; core-sections-navigation triggers normal browser navigation to the mapped URL.
5. The target page loads and implements its own functionality (outside this page’s scope).

Workflow: Use in-page navigation with state reflected in URL
1. User is on about.html and sees an in-page navigation list for sections such as mission, services, contact, policies, and core sections.
2. The user clicks a navigation item (e.g., “Policies & Terms”).
3. about-page-internal-nav calls the browser history API to set the URL to about.html?section=privacy_terms (or another appropriate value) without a full reload.
4. about-page-anchor-router updates the in-memory selectedSection state and instructs the view to scroll to the policies-section and optionally highlight it.
5. If the user reloads the page, the same section parameter is read again, reconstructing the in-page scroll position and focus after getAlumniOfficeInfo completes.
