interactions: Clicking an in-page navigation item updates the section URL parameter using pushState/replaceState and scrolls to the corresponding section component.
Clicking “Email newsletters” inside or near the services section navigates to newsletter_subscribe.html?source=about_page using a standard link or programmatic navigation.

Clicking a core section entry (from core_sections) navigates to the mapped target page (e.g., events.html, give.html, chapters.html, benefits.html, alumni_directory.html, mentoring.html, scholarships.html) based on the entry’s code.
Clicking email addresses in the contact section opens the user’s default email client via mailto: links generated from contact_information.email_addresses.
Clicking phone numbers in the contact section initiates a call via tel: links, constructed from contact_information.phone_numbers.
Clicking within the policies section on a policy header (privacy, terms of use, accessibility) toggles its open/closed state (e.g., accordion behavior) within the policies-section component.

Loading the page with a section URL parameter (e.g., section=privacy_terms, section=mission, section=contact, section=services, section=core_sections) causes about-page-anchor-router to mark the matching section as active and scroll to it once data has loaded.

If getAlumniOfficeInfo fails to load (e.g., network error), the about-office-data-loader triggers an error state interaction, displaying a generic error message that does not hardcode any mission, services, or policy content, and may offer a retry action.
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary external state for this page is the section URL parameter. On initial load, about-page-anchor-router reads window.location.search and extracts the section value. Valid example values include mission, services, contact, privacy_terms, and core_sections (additional values can be supported as needed). This section value is stored in an in-memory state variable, such as selectedSection. The about-office-data-loader then calls getAlumniOfficeInfo and, once data is available, the selectedSection state is used to determine which component should be scrolled into view and, in the case of privacy_terms, which part of the policies-section should be expanded and focused. When the user clicks an in-page navigation link, the page updates the section parameter using history.pushState or history.replaceState, without reloading, and updates selectedSection accordingly. No state is persisted using localStorage or similar mechanisms; all non-URL state (such as which policy panels are expanded, or whether data is currently loading) is held only in memory for the lifetime of the page session. If the user reloads the page, the state is reconstructed solely from the current URL, and getAlumniOfficeInfo is called again. All content details (mission, services descriptions, contact info, policies, and core section names/descriptions) originate exclusively from the getAlumniOfficeInfo response and are not hardcoded in the page.

components.functionality: On page initialization, call the getAlumniOfficeInfo interface with no parameters, manage loading and error states, and distribute the returned mission, services, contact_information, policies, and core_sections data to child components via a shared state or context.

Parse the section URL parameter on load and on history changes, maintain an in-memory selectedSection state, and coordinate scrolling/focusing behavior for the corresponding content section (mission, services, contact, policies/privacy_terms, or core sections). When triggered by in-page navigation, update the section parameter in the URL using the History API without hardcoding any content values from the API.

Render the alumni office mission text using the mission value obtained from getAlumniOfficeInfo. If mission is absent or empty in the API response, the component can choose to hide itself or show a generic non-content-specific notice, but it must not inject any hardcoded mission text.

Display the list of alumni office services using the services array from getAlumniOfficeInfo. Each service object’s title and description are rendered as-is from the API. Additionally, the component includes a dedicated action element (such as a button or link) labeled for email newsletters that, when clicked, navigates to newsletter_subscribe.html?source=about_page. The component does not hardcode any of the service titles or descriptions themselves.

Render the alumni office contact information using contact_information from getAlumniOfficeInfo. This includes looping through contact_information.email_addresses to create mailto: links, looping through contact_information.phone_numbers to create tel: links, and displaying contact_information.mailing_address. If any of these fields are missing in the response, the corresponding sub-section is omitted rather than filled with hardcoded placeholder values.

Present policy summaries using the policies object from getAlumniOfficeInfo. It exposes separate accordion items or subsections for privacy, terms of use, and accessibility, bound respectively to policies.privacy_summary, policies.terms_of_use_summary, and policies.accessibility_summary. When the page is loaded with section=privacy_terms, this component automatically expands and focuses the privacy and terms-related items. It tracks which subsections are open in memory only, without persisting that state outside the page.

Render a list of navigable entries for core alumni site sections using the core_sections array from getAlumniOfficeInfo. Each entry uses core_sections[i].title and core_sections[i].description for display, and core_sections[i].code to determine the destination URL. The mapping from code to URL is implemented as routing logic (e.g., events → events.html, make_a_gift → give.html, chapters → chapters.html, benefits → benefits.html, directory → alumni_directory.html, mentoring → mentoring.html, scholarships → scholarships.html) and does not involve hardcoded versions of any titles or descriptions.

Provide a compact navigation control that lets users jump between the main sections of the page (mission, services, contact, policies, and core sections). Each item, when clicked, calls about-page-anchor-router to update the section URL parameter and initiate scrolling to the target section’s anchor. This component does not render any data from getAlumniOfficeInfo; it is purely navigational and uses stable, implementation-defined anchor identifiers that are not tied to content values.

Display loading and error states for the About page. While getAlumniOfficeInfo is in progress, it can show a generic loading indicator (without content-specific text). If onLoadError is fired by about-office-data-loader, it shows a general error message and exposes a retry control that triggers a new call to getAlumniOfficeInfo. It never hardcodes any mission, services, or policy text.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column desktop grid with a full-width header and navigation, and a centered content column spanning approximately 6–8 columns with large side gutters.
layout.layout_pattern: Below the existing global red utility bar and black main navigation, the page switches into an extremely simple, editorial single-column layout. All About-page components are stacked vertically in one centered column that feels like reading a formal letter: page heading, brief intro, in-page navigation, then mission, services, contact, policies, and finally the core sections navigation. There are no sidebars or split columns; all information is presented in a linear top-to-bottom flow, keeping the user focused on the content in the middle of the screen.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Below the existing global red utility bar and black main navigation, the page switches into an extremely simple, editorial single-column layout. All About-page components are stacked vertically in one centered column that feels like reading a formal letter: page heading, brief intro, in-page navigation, then mission, services, contact, policies, and finally the core sections navigation. There are no sidebars or split columns; all information is presented in a linear top-to-bottom flow, keeping the user focused on the content in the middle of the screen.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The global header and navigation together occupy roughly the top 20–25% of the viewport. Directly beneath, a large white gap (about 64px–80px) creates breathing room before the About content begins. The main content column is centered and relatively narrow, taking up about 35–45% of the viewport width on desktop, with generous left and right gutters. Each major section (mission, services, contact, policies, core sections navigation) is separated by 32–48px of vertical space. The status banner, when present, occupies a slim horizontal band across the content column, just above the page title.
layout_strategies.content_density: The content density is low and editorial. Text is presented in short paragraphs with comfortable line height and 16–24px vertical spacing between paragraphs. Headings are modest but clearly separated from body text. The accordion for policies uses full-width rows with ample padding, avoiding visual noise. Navigation lists (in-page nav and core sections navigation) are light-weight text links with sufficient spacing, keeping the page from feeling crowded. Overall, the design prioritizes readability and calm over compactness.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The eye travels from the bright red utility bar at the top, down to the black main navigation bar. From there, it drops into the white content area, first catching the status banner (if active), then the clear page heading. Immediately below, the in-page navigation offers quick anchors but remains visually subordinate to the heading. The user then scrolls down through mission and services in a steady vertical rhythm, reaching contact information as a practical reference block. The policies accordion introduces slight interactivity but still follows the same vertical flow. Finally, the core sections navigation appears as a standalone list near the bottom, acting like the LinkedIn focal line in the original mockup—an obvious pathway out to the rest of the alumni site before the page ends.

layout.overall_description: On desktop, the page begins with a full-width red utility bar and a black main navigation bar, matching the institutional, slightly modern corporate style. Below these, the screen opens into a wide white canvas. Centered within this canvas is a relatively narrow column—about 40% of the viewport width—that reads like a formal letter. After a generous vertical gap from the navigation, a slim status banner (if present) runs across this column, followed by a restrained "About the Alumni Office" heading and a short introductory paragraph. Just beneath, a simple in-page navigation row lists the main sections (Mission, Services, Contact, Policies), functioning as subtle anchors without breaking the single-column feel. As the user scrolls, content unfolds in a clean vertical stack: the mission section, then services, then a compact contact info panel, then a full-width policies accordion. Each section is clearly separated with consistent vertical spacing and alignment to the same central column, maintaining a steady reading rhythm. Near the bottom, the core sections navigation appears as a minimal, standalone list of key alumni site links, echoing the focal LinkedIn line from the mockup and providing a natural exit point. The overall impression is of a formal, letter-like document framed by institutional chrome at the top, with no secondary columns or distractions.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This component has no persistent visible presence in the final layout. Functionally, it sits at the top of the About page content column, just below the main navigation region in the DOM hierarchy. While data is loading, it temporarily occupies the central content column (the same 35–45% viewport width, centered) with either a single-line loading message or a small spinner and text, vertically centered within the first 25–35% of the white canvas beneath the nav bars. Once data is loaded, its visual footprint disappears and the regular content stack (status banner, heading, in-page nav, sections) takes its place.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual in steady state; only takes over the central content column during loading, displacing the other components downward until loading completes.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This state controller is invisible and does not render any visible UI elements. It is conceptually attached to the main content column, managing scroll behavior and deep links to sections like Mission, Services, Contact, and Policies. It aligns logically with the in-page navigation and section headings but does not occupy its own space; rather, it coordinates scrolling to the top edges of those sections within the same central column.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual. It influences scroll position and URL anchors but does not affect spatial measurements or introduce any visible elements.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The mission section is the first major content block in the main column. It is aligned to the center of the viewport, restricted to about 40% of the viewport width, with large white gutters on both sides. Vertically, it sits directly beneath the in-page navigation, separated from it by about 24–32px of space. At the top of this block, a left-aligned section title (e.g., “Mission”) appears in a slightly larger, bold text, followed by a 16–24px gap before the body copy begins. The mission text is broken into short paragraphs with about 16–24px of vertical spacing between them, creating a calm, letter-like feel. The bottom of the mission section ends with a clear 32–40px gap before the services section begins, maintaining the stacked editorial rhythm.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block-level section in the central column; it grows vertically with content but always respects the fixed centered width and consistent margins.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The services section is positioned immediately below the mission section within the same centered content column, separated by about 32–40px of white space. It mirrors the mission section’s width, alignment, and typographic style, keeping everything within the 35–45% viewport-width column. A left-aligned section heading (e.g., “Services”) sits at the top of the block, aligned exactly with the mission heading above, followed by a 16–24px gap. The services content is presented in either short paragraphs or a simple vertical list, with each item separated by roughly 12–16px of vertical space. There are no columns or cards; all service descriptions flow in a single vertical stack. A clear 32–40px gap at the bottom differentiates services from the contact info panel that follows.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block-level section; expands vertically with content but remains within the central editorial column with fixed alignment.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contact info panel sits directly below the services section, separated by about 32–40px, and continues the same central, single-column layout. It shares the same width (about 40% of viewport, centered) and left-aligned text baseline. At its top, a modest “Contact” or “Contact Information” heading aligns with the headings above, followed by a 16–24px gap. Below this, key contact details (address, phone, email, office hours) are stacked as short lines or tightly grouped paragraphs, with 8–16px spacing between items. The overall block remains visually simple and text-only, resembling a formal letter’s contact block. The bottom of this panel is followed by another 32–40px vertical gap before the policies accordion begins, so the user clearly perceives it as a distinct, practical section.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static section anchored in the central column; no special behavior beyond growing vertically with content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The policies section appears below the contact info panel, separated by about 32–40px of whitespace, and continues to use the same centered column width. A left-aligned heading (e.g., “Key Policies”) sits at the top, aligned with previous headings, with a 16–24px gap beneath. Below the heading, a vertical stack of accordion items fills the width of the column. Each accordion row is a full-width bar within the column, about 48–64px tall when collapsed, with 12–16px internal vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal padding on both left and right. Policy titles align left; a simple disclosure icon (e.g., chevron) aligns to the right edge, maintaining the clean institutional style. When an accordion item is expanded, its body text opens directly beneath the title within the same bar, pushing subsequent items downward while still constrained to the central column. There is a 16–24px gap between each accordion item, and a final 32–40px gap below the last item before the core sections navigation begins.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static vertical stack; accordion panels expand and collapse in place, shifting subsequent content downward but never breaking the single-column centered alignment.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The core sections navigation is positioned near the bottom of the content column, directly under the policies section, separated from it by about 32–40px of space. It acts as the final focal element before the page ends, similar to the standalone LinkedIn link moment in the mockup. The navigation list is contained within the same 35–45% viewport-width central column. A subtle heading like “Explore Core Alumni Sections” may appear at the top, left-aligned with previous headings, or the list can be standalone if a heading is not used. Below, each core section link (e.g., Alumni Network, Events, Career Services) is presented as a left-aligned, single-line text link spanning the width of the column, with 12–16px vertical spacing between links. There are no icons or heavy buttons; links rely on typography and subtle hover states. A generous 32–48px bottom margin after the final link provides a calm visual conclusion to the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static navigation list; remains anchored at the bottom of the central column and scrolls away naturally with the page.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The in-page navigation sits near the top of the white content area, inside the central column, just below the main page heading and any short introductory paragraph. From the bottom of the header/nav region, there is first a large 64–80px gap, then the page heading, a 16–24px gap to an optional 1–2 line intro, and then another 16–24px gap before the in-page nav. The in-page nav itself spans the full width of the content column (about 40% of viewport, centered) and is left-aligned. It appears as a horizontal row of 3–4 text links (e.g., Mission, Services, Contact, Policies) separated by 16–24px, or by slim vertical dividers. The styling is understated—similar to small nav tabs but without heavy borders—so it supports navigation without overpowering the editorial feel. On narrower screens, these links can wrap onto two lines while still aligned to the left within the central column.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static; scrolls with the content. It serves as anchor links to sections managed by the anchor router but does not stick to the top of the viewport.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The status banner appears as a slim horizontal band within the white content area, just under the global header and navigation but above the page heading. After the black main navigation ends, there is a modest 24–32px white gap, then the status banner is centered on the page, matching the main content column width (about 35–45% of viewport). The banner has a subtle background tint (for example, a very light red or gray) with 8–12px of vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal padding inside, and rounded corners kept minimal to align with the conservative, flat design. Its text is left-aligned within the band and kept to one or two lines. Below the banner, a 24–32px gap leads into the main page heading, reinforcing the sense that the banner is a brief, contextual notice rather than a core content block.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static and inline with the content column; appears only when there is a status to show, pushing the rest of the content downward but not fixed or sticky.
